data.title: Input Layer and Encoding
data.description: Encode arithmetic expressions and linear equations as directed graphs. No node attributes — identity emerges from structure. Input layer immutable. Seed rules provided as bootstrap scaffold.
data.exit_condition: Expressions reliably converted to base graphs. Sample manually inspected and encoding confirmed sane.

encoding.numbers: Open-length binary tree. MSB at root, LSB at deepest right leaf. Leaf nodes encoded by tag: empty node = 0, single self-loop = 1. Internal nodes have exactly two structural children (left = higher bits, right = current bit). Each number has exactly one structural representation.
encoding.operators: Operator node with a cycle-based structural tag. +: triangle (3-cycle). -: triangle + tail node. *: square (4-cycle). /: square + tail node. Operator node connects to its tag via a structural edge. Operands connected via operational edges (left first, right second).
encoding.equality: Two distinct structural families. Unfinished equality (open equation, at least one parameter): pentagon (5-cycle) tag. Finished equality (both sides concrete, terminal state): pentagon + tail node tag. Rules only match unfinished equality nodes — finished equality is the output state, no rules fire on it. Transition from unfinished to finished is the reduction completion marker.
encoding.variable_x: Parameter node with bidirectional structural edge to a dedicated companion node. All parameters are structurally identical — identity from position in expression tree. Arithmetic rules do not match parameter nodes by structural non-match.
encoding.structural_families: Five families: number internals (binary branching), number leaves (empty or self-loop), operators (cycle tags of distinct size/shape), parameters (bidirectional companion), equality (5-cycle unfinished / 5-cycle+tail finished). Families are mutually non-isomorphic at local topology.
encoding.expression_tree: Operators as internal nodes, numbers and variables as leaves. Tree topology encodes evaluation order. No explicit bracket nodes needed.
encoding.carry_propagation: Operational edge from operator to current bit position acts as cursor. Rule detaches and reattaches edge to next bit node. No extra marker node required.

label_format.arithmetic: Terminal value node only — e.g. 7. No wrapper.
label_format.equations: Solved form graph — e.g. x = 3. Irreducible — x = 3 does not collapse to 3.
label_format.collapse_rule: x = x collapses to x. 3 = 3 handled by finished equality recognition, not a rewrite rule. x = 3 does not collapse — both sides are structurally distinct.

neutral_element_collapses: add_zero_collapse: x + 0 -> x (pattern matches + node with one operational edge to zero; surviving operand recovered dynamically)
zero_product_collapses: mul_zero_collapse: x * 0 -> 0 (pattern matches * node with one operational edge to zero; whole subtree replaced by fresh zero node)
seed_rules.notes: Rules are partial pattern matches — neutral element pattern covers only the operator node and the neutral operand subgraph. Surviving operand not in the pattern, recovered from live graph in rewrite lambda. No wildcard match extension to OperationDefinition needed for these rules.

complexity_ladder: Level 2: One-step solving — e.g. x + 4 = 7, label: x = 3
Level 3: Two-step solving — e.g. 2x + 4 = 10, label: x = 3
Level 4: Variable on both sides — e.g. 2x + 4 = 3x - 1, label: x = 5
deferred_decisions: 3 = 3 collapse: not a rewrite rule. Handled by finished equality recognition — if both operand subtrees are concrete values, equality is already finished. Structural isomorphism check deferred; may not be needed within current complexity ladder scope.
Wildcard match in OperationDefinition: not needed for P2 seed rules. May be revisited for higher-level rules.
Negative number encoding: not in P2 scope.
